title: C# HTTP Client TypeSpec Emitter Rewrite
description: Rewrite the C# HTTP client code generator from a two-phase system (TypeScript emitter → JSON → C# Roslyn generator) to a single-phase TypeScript emitter using the Alloy JSX framework. The rewrite must produce identical C# output to the legacy generator.

phases.title: Project Foundation
phases.description: Set up the emitter entry point, System.ClientModel builtins, root output component, and testing infrastructure. This is the foundation that all subsequent phases build upon.

phases.title: Type System — Models and Enums
phases.description: Generate C# model classes, enum types, and their serialization companions from TCGC types.

phases.title: JSON Serialization (IJsonModel / IPersistableModel)
phases.description: Generate the .Serialization.cs partial classes implementing JSON serialization and deserialization for all model types.

phases.title: Client Generation
phases.description: Generate client classes, REST clients, protocol methods, and convenience methods.

phases.title: Paging and Long-Running Operations
phases.description: Generate paging collection classes and LRO support.

phases.title: Infrastructure Helpers
phases.description: Generate the ~15 internal infrastructure files that support the client library.

phases.title: XML Serialization (Conditional)
phases.description: Generate XML serialization support for models with XML usage.

phases.title: JSON Merge Patch / Dynamic Models (Conditional)
phases.description: Generate JsonPatch-aware models for merge-patch scenarios.

phases.title: Project Scaffolding
phases.description: Generate .csproj and .sln files for new projects.

phases.title: Multipart Form Data (Conditional)
phases.description: Generate multipart content handling for file upload operations.

phases.title: Integration Testing and Validation
phases.description: End-to-end validation against legacy generator golden files.

phases.title: Smoke Test Build Failures
phases.description: Issues discovered by smoke-testing the emitter output with `dotnet build`. The smoke tests compile TypeSpec fixtures from `test/fixtures/smoke/` and run `dotnet build` on the generated output in `temp/smoke/`. These tasks fix the C# compilation errors found in the generated code. Run `pnpm test -- test/smoke.test.ts` to verify fixes.

phases.title: E2E Spector Test Failures
phases.description: Issues discovered by the Spector e2e test infrastructure (test/e2e/). The e2e tests compile TypeSpec http-specs through the new emitter, write C# client projects to temp/e2e/Spector/http/, then build and run the legacy Spector NUnit tests against a live mock server. These tasks track generation failures, build errors in generated code, and test failures. Run `pnpm test:e2e` to reproduce all issues. Run `npx tsx test/e2e/generate.ts --clean` to regenerate, then `dotnet build test/e2e/Spector.Tests/Spector.Tests.csproj` to see build errors.

tasks.title: Fix rootNamespace captured before cleanAllNamespaces causing internal helper namespace mismatch
tasks.description:
In emitter.tsx, rootNamespace is resolved at line 152 BEFORE cleanAllNamespaces() runs at line 163. Internal helper files (Argument.cs, ClientUriBuilder.cs, ClientPipelineExtensions.cs, CancellationTokenExtensions.cs, etc.) use the pre-clean rootNamespace for their namespace, while client files use the post-clean namespace from mutated SdkClientType objects. When cleanAllNamespaces prefixes a segment with underscore (e.g., MoveMethodParameterToClient → _MoveMethodParameterToClient), the internal helpers end up in a different namespace than the client code, causing CS0103 (Argument not found, ~82 errors), CS0246 (ClientUriBuilder not found, ~44 errors), CS1061 (ToRequestOptions/ProcessMessage not found, ~88 errors). Fix by resolving rootNamespace after cleanAllNamespaces, or re-deriving it from the cleaned first client namespace.

Affected specs: azure/client-generator-core/client-location/*, and any spec where cleanAllNamespaces modifies the root client namespace.

To reproduce: pnpm test:e2e, then check dotnet build errors for 'Argument', 'ClientUriBuilder', 'ToRequestOptions', 'ProcessMessage'.
acceptance_criteria: Internal helper files and client files share the same root namespace after cleanAllNamespaces
CS0103 (Argument), CS0246 (ClientUriBuilder), CS1061 (ToRequestOptions, ProcessMessage) errors are eliminated
azure/client-generator-core/client-location specs compile without namespace mismatch errors

tasks.title: Fix C# reserved keyword escaping in generated parameter names
tasks.description:
The special-words Spector spec generates C# code with C# reserved keywords (continue, break, class, async, await, etc.) used as parameter and variable names without the required @ prefix. This produces ~1052 syntax errors (CS1002, CS1513, CS1026, CS1001, CS1003) in the special-words spec alone. The Alloy C# naming policy should escape these, but it appears to be bypassed for some code paths (e.g., raw string interpolation in RestClient request-building methods, parameter declarations in protocol/convenience methods).

Task 12.2.2 was previously marked done but the fix is incomplete — the escaping works for some paths but not all. Audit all code paths that emit parameter names (ProtocolMethod, ConvenienceMethod, RestClientFile CreateRequest methods) and ensure they pass through the C# naming policy or explicitly apply @ prefix escaping.

To reproduce: pnpm test:e2e, check errors in special-words/src/Generated/Parameters.RestClient.cs.
acceptance_criteria: special-words spec compiles without syntax errors from reserved keyword parameter names
All parameter names that are C# reserved keywords are prefixed with @ in generated code
Existing unit and scenario tests still pass

tasks.title: Fix hyphenated HTTP header parameter names not converted to valid C# identifiers
tasks.description:
HTTP header parameter names containing hyphens (e.g., x-ms-test-header) are emitted as-is in generated C# code instead of being converted to valid camelCase identifiers (e.g., xMsTestHeader). This produces CS0103 errors ('x', 'ms', 'test', 'header' not found, ~192 errors) because C# interprets hyphens as minus operators. The issue appears in convenience method bodies where parameter names from the operation are used directly without applying the C# naming policy.

Task 12.2.4 was previously marked done but the fix is incomplete — the naming conversion works for parameter declarations but not for parameter usage sites in method bodies (argument validation, protocol method call arguments).

Affected specs: parameters/spread (Alias.cs), documentation, and others with header parameters.

To reproduce: pnpm test:e2e, check errors in parameters/spread/src/Generated/Alias.cs around x-ms-test-header.
acceptance_criteria: Hyphenated parameter names are converted to valid camelCase C# identifiers in all usage sites
CS0103 errors for split hyphenated names (x, ms, test, header) are eliminated
parameters/spread spec compiles without identifier errors

tasks.title: Add missing using System directive in ModelFactory generated files
tasks.description:
ModelFactoryFile.tsx (line 79) hardcodes using={['System.Collections.Generic', 'System.Linq']} without including 'System'. When factory method parameters include types from the System namespace (DateTimeOffset, TimeSpan, Uri), the generated code fails with CS0246 (~41 errors: DateTimeOffset 13, TimeSpan 18, Uri 10).

The fix is straightforward: add 'System' to the using list in ModelFactoryFile.tsx. Alternatively, rely on Alloy's automatic using generation by ensuring TypeExpression resolves these types through the library reference system.

Affected specs: azure/client-generator-core/client-initialization/default, individually, individuallyParent, and others with DateTimeOffset/TimeSpan/Uri model properties.

To reproduce: pnpm test:e2e, check CS0246 errors for DateTimeOffset in ModelFactory files.
acceptance_criteria: Generated ModelFactory files include using System; when System types are used
CS0246 errors for DateTimeOffset, TimeSpan, Uri in ModelFactory files are eliminated
pnpm test passes (unit + scenario tests)

tasks.title: Fix Type namespace segment shadowing System.Type in internal helper files
tasks.description:
Internal helper files (e.g., CodeGenSuppressAttribute.cs) reference System.Type, but when the generated namespace contains a 'Type' segment (e.g., Type.Array namespace), the Type segment shadows System.Type, producing CS0118 errors (~36 occurrences: 'Type is a namespace but is used like a type').

The fix should either: (a) use global::System.Type in internal helper templates that reference System.Type, or (b) ensure the namespace cleaning in cleanAllNamespaces also applies to the rootNamespace used by internal files (related to task 12.9).

Affected specs: type/array, type/dictionary, type/enum/*, type/file, type/model/*, type/property/*, type/scalar, type/union/*.

To reproduce: pnpm test:e2e, check CS0118 errors in Internal/CodeGenSuppressAttribute.cs.
acceptance_criteria: Internal helper files using System.Type compile even when namespace contains a Type segment
CS0118 errors for Type namespace/type collision are eliminated
type/* specs compile without namespace collision errors

tasks.title: Fix RequestOptions-to-CancellationToken argument conversion errors in convenience methods
tasks.description:
Generated convenience methods pass RequestOptions where CancellationToken is expected, producing CS1503 errors (~56 occurrences: 'cannot convert from RequestOptions to CancellationToken'). This indicates the convenience method body is calling a protocol overload that expects RequestOptions but passing CancellationToken-derived arguments, or vice versa.

Investigation needed: check how convenience methods build their call to protocol methods, particularly the argument conversion logic in ConvenienceMethod.tsx. The convenience method should call the protocol overload with RequestOptions (converting CancellationToken via ToRequestOptions extension), not the convenience overload.

Affected specs: multiple specs with convenience methods.

To reproduce: pnpm test:e2e, check CS1503 errors involving RequestOptions/CancellationToken conversion.
acceptance_criteria: Convenience methods correctly convert CancellationToken to RequestOptions when calling protocol methods
CS1503 errors for RequestOptions/CancellationToken conversion are eliminated

tasks.title: Clean stale output from testignored specs in emit-e2e script
tasks.description:
The emit-e2e.ts script skips specs listed in .testignore during emission but does not clean their previously generated output directories. The generateProjectReferences function then scans all .csproj files in the output directory (including stale ones from ignored specs) and includes them in GeneratedProjectReferences.props. This causes dotnet build to attempt compiling stale code from ignored specs, producing ~56 errors from old ARM resource-manager output.

Fix options: (a) have emit-e2e.ts clean output directories for ignored specs, (b) exclude ignored spec directories when generating project references, or (c) both.

To reproduce: pnpm test:e2e with ARM specs in .testignore, observe CS errors from azure/resource-manager/* paths.
acceptance_criteria: Stale output from testignored specs is either cleaned or excluded from project references
dotnet build does not attempt to compile code from ignored specs
No errors from azure/resource-manager/* paths when those specs are in .testignore

tasks.title: Fix CollectionResult constructor parameter mismatch for paging methods
tasks.description:
CollectionResultFile.tsx only populates operation parameters for continuation-token paging, but PagingMethods.tsx always passes all operation parameters in the constructor call. For next-link paging, the constructor has fewer parameters than the caller provides, causing CS1729 (wrong constructor argument count), CS7036 (missing required parameter), and cascading CS1061 (missing member) errors.

The fix should include operation parameters for both continuation-token AND next-link paging strategies in CollectionResultFile.tsx, and align the CreateXxxRequest parameter lists between RestClient and CollectionResult.

Affected specs: azure/core/basic, azure/core/page, azure/payload/pageable, payload/pageable, azure/client-generator-core/next-link-verb.

To reproduce: Remove these specs from test/e2e/.testignore, run pnpm emit:e2e --filter azure/core/basic, then dotnet build.
acceptance_criteria: CollectionResult constructors accept all operation parameters for both next-link and continuation-token paging
CreateXxxRequest parameter lists align between RestClient and CollectionResult
CS1729, CS7036, CS1061 errors for paging specs are eliminated
azure/core/basic, azure/core/page, azure/payload/pageable, payload/pageable, azure/client-generator-core/next-link-verb compile without errors

tasks.title: Fix IJsonModel<T> declared on non-JSON models (multipart, file)
tasks.description:
ModelSerializationFile.tsx declares IJsonModel<T> interface on models that don't have JSON serialization (multipart form data models, file upload models). The interface is declared but JsonModelWriteCore and JsonModelCreateCore methods are not generated, causing CS0535 errors (56+ occurrences).

The fix should either: (a) not declare IJsonModel<T> for models whose usage doesn't include JSON serialization, or (b) generate stub JsonModelWriteCore/JsonModelCreateCore methods that throw NotSupportedException.

Also has secondary CS0104 issue: generated type name 'File' collides with System.IO.File in payload/multipart.

Affected specs: payload/multipart, type/file, azure/client-generator-core/override.

To reproduce: Remove these specs from test/e2e/.testignore, run pnpm emit:e2e --filter payload/multipart, then dotnet build.
acceptance_criteria: Models without JSON serialization usage do not declare IJsonModel<T> or generate stub implementations
CS0535 errors for IJsonModel Write/Create methods are eliminated
payload/multipart, type/file, azure/client-generator-core/override compile without errors

tasks.title: Fix missing using directives for System types in generated code
tasks.description:
Generated code references types (IDictionary<,>, DateTimeOffset, TimeSpan, Dictionary<,>, List<>, ClientUriBuilder) without corresponding using statements. Type references are constructed as plain strings rather than through Alloy's refkey system which auto-generates using directives.

Specific issues:
- type/dictionary: IDictionary<,> not found in model property declarations (~44 errors)
- type/property/nullable: DateTimeOffset, TimeSpan not found (~12 errors)
- payload/json-merge-patch: Dictionary<,>, IDictionary<,>, List<> not found (~14 errors)
- service/multi-service: ClientUriBuilder not found, missing extension methods (~26 errors)
- client/structure/client-operation-group: ClientUriBuilder, Argument not found (~26 errors)

The fix should ensure all type references go through proper Alloy JSX TypeExpression components or add explicit using directives.

To reproduce: Remove these specs from test/e2e/.testignore, run pnpm emit:e2e --filter type/dictionary, then dotnet build.
acceptance_criteria: All generated type references include proper using directives
CS0246 errors for IDictionary, DateTimeOffset, TimeSpan, Dictionary, List, ClientUriBuilder are eliminated
type/dictionary, type/property/nullable, payload/json-merge-patch, service/multi-service, client/structure/client-operation-group compile without errors

tasks.title: Add namespace collision detection for generated type names
tasks.description:
Generated type names collide with system types or namespaces, causing ambiguous reference errors:
- CS0104: 'Enum' ambiguous between generated _Specs_.Azure.Example.Basic.Enum and System.Enum (azure/example/basic)
- CS0104: 'Object' ambiguous between generated union Object type and System.Object (type/union/discriminated)
- CS0104: 'ContinuationToken' ambiguous between generated and System.ClientModel types (payload/pageable)
- CS0118: 'Type' namespace segment shadows System.Type (type/property/optionality)

The fix should either: (a) add global:: qualification for system type references when collisions exist, (b) rename generated types that collide with well-known system types, or (c) use explicit namespace aliasing in the generated code.

Affected specs: azure/example/basic, type/union/discriminated, type/property/optionality.

To reproduce: Remove these specs from test/e2e/.testignore, run pnpm emit:e2e --filter azure/example/basic, then dotnet build.
acceptance_criteria: Generated type names that collide with system types are disambiguated
CS0104 errors for Enum, Object, ContinuationToken, File ambiguities are eliminated
CS0118 errors for Type namespace/type collision are eliminated
azure/example/basic, type/union/discriminated, type/property/optionality compile without errors

tasks.title: Fix type mapping in serialization constructors for inherited models
tasks.description:
Deserialization constructors and model factory methods pass arguments in wrong order or with wrong types for inherited models. Specific issues:
- CS1503: Uri passed where DateTime expected (deserialize-empty-string-as-null)
- CS1503: int passed where IDictionary<string, BinaryData> expected — model factory argument order mismatch for inherited models (not-discriminated, nested-discriminator, hierarchy-building)
- CS1503: Wrong scalar type in deserialization for various type conversions (type/scalar, routes, type/model/visibility)
- CS1503: RequestOptions passed where CancellationToken expected (routes) — convenience method calls protocol method with wrong argument types

The root cause for inheritance models is that the model factory passes arguments in property order, but the serialization constructor has additionalBinaryDataProperties interleaved between base and own properties.

Affected specs: azure/client-generator-core/deserialize-empty-string-as-null, azure/client-generator-core/hierarchy-building, type/model/inheritance/enum-discriminator, type/model/inheritance/nested-discriminator, type/model/inheritance/not-discriminated, type/model/visibility, type/scalar, routes.

To reproduce: Remove these specs from test/e2e/.testignore, run pnpm emit:e2e --filter type/model/inheritance/not-discriminated, then dotnet build.
acceptance_criteria: Model factory argument order matches serialization constructor parameter order for all inheritance patterns
Scalar type conversions in deserialization match the property type (Uri, DateTime, etc.)
Convenience methods pass correct argument types to protocol methods
CS1503 errors for type mismatches in serialization are eliminated
All 8 affected specs compile without errors

tasks.title: Fix non-static member access in recursive model serialization
tasks.description:
In type/model/inheritance/recursive, the Extension model's serialization code accesses Element.Extension as if it were a static member from a static DeserializeExtension method, causing CS0120 errors (6 occurrences).

The generated code does something like:
  Element.Extension (accessing instance property Extension on class Element)
instead of properly accessing the deserialized result.

The fix needs to ensure the deserialization code for recursive models correctly handles self-referential property access patterns.

Affected spec: type/model/inheritance/recursive.

To reproduce: Remove this spec from test/e2e/.testignore, run pnpm emit:e2e --filter type/model/inheritance/recursive, then dotnet build.
acceptance_criteria: Recursive model serialization does not access instance members from static context
CS0120 errors for Element.Extension are eliminated
type/model/inheritance/recursive compiles without errors

tasks.title: Fix property name collision with enclosing class name
tasks.description:
In the special-words spec, a model class SameAsModel has a property also named SameAsModel, violating C# rule CS0542 (member names cannot be the same as their enclosing type). The naming policy should detect this collision and rename the property (e.g., append 'Property' suffix or use a different naming strategy).

Affected spec: special-words.

To reproduce: Remove this spec from test/e2e/.testignore, run pnpm emit:e2e --filter special-words, then dotnet build.
acceptance_criteria: Properties named the same as their enclosing class are renamed to avoid CS0542
special-words spec compiles without member name collision errors

tasks.title: Fix missing Argument helper class reference in LRO models
tasks.description:
Models in azure/core/lro/rpc and azure/core/lro/standard reference Argument.AssertNotNull() but the Argument helper class is not in scope (CS0103, 6 occurrences). The internal helper files are generated but the using directive for the Internal namespace is missing from these model files.

This may be related to task 12.9 (rootNamespace mismatch) — if the Internal namespace differs from what the model expects.

Affected specs: azure/core/lro/rpc, azure/core/lro/standard.

To reproduce: Remove these specs from test/e2e/.testignore, run pnpm emit:e2e --filter azure/core/lro/rpc, then dotnet build.
acceptance_criteria: Models using Argument.AssertNotNull() have the correct using directive for the Internal namespace
CS0103 errors for Argument not found are eliminated
azure/core/lro/rpc, azure/core/lro/standard compile without errors

tasks.title: Fix convenience method overload argument count mismatches
tasks.description:
Generated convenience methods call protocol method overloads with wrong argument counts, causing CS1501 errors:
- client/overload: CreateGetAllRequest called with 2 args but no matching overload (4 errors)
- azure/core/traits: Convenience method passes wrong number of arguments to protocol method (4 errors)
- special-headers/conditional-request: Similar argument count mismatch (4 errors)

The issue is in how ConvenienceMethod.tsx builds the call to the protocol method — it doesn't always match the protocol method's parameter list when there are optional parameters or method overloads.

Affected specs: client/overload, azure/core/traits, special-headers/conditional-request.

To reproduce: Remove these specs from test/e2e/.testignore, run pnpm emit:e2e --filter client/overload, then dotnet build.
acceptance_criteria: Convenience method calls match the protocol method's expected parameter count
CS1501 errors for method argument count mismatches are eliminated
client/overload, azure/core/traits, special-headers/conditional-request compile without errors

tasks.title: Fix union type serialization and conversion methods
tasks.description:
Union type serialization generates incorrect code:
- CS1929: BinaryData.ToSerialString() — ToSerialString is an extension method for the union enum type, not BinaryData (4 errors)
- CS0029: Cannot implicitly convert union enum types (EnumsOnlyCasesLr, EnumsOnlyCasesUd) to BinaryData (4 errors)

The serialization code tries to serialize union values using methods that don't exist on the generated types. The union type expression and serialization logic needs to handle the case where a union value needs to be serialized through its discriminated type.

Affected spec: type/union.

To reproduce: Remove this spec from test/e2e/.testignore, run pnpm emit:e2e --filter type/union, then dotnet build.
acceptance_criteria: Union type values are serialized using correct methods for their discriminated type
CS1929 errors for missing extension methods on union types are eliminated
CS0029 errors for union type conversion are eliminated
type/union compiles without errors

tasks.title: Fix spread parameter model constructor missing additionalBinaryDataProperties
tasks.description:
In the parameters/spread spec, the generated code for alias operations creates SpreadWithMultipleParametersRequest instances without passing the required additionalBinaryDataProperties parameter (CS7036, 4 errors). The convenience method or rest client code that constructs the spread model doesn't include the synthetic additionalBinaryDataProperties argument.

Affected spec: parameters/spread.

To reproduce: Remove this spec from test/e2e/.testignore, run pnpm emit:e2e --filter parameters/spread, then dotnet build.
acceptance_criteria: Spread model instantiation includes all required constructor parameters including additionalBinaryDataProperties
CS7036 errors for missing required parameters are eliminated
parameters/spread compiles without errors

tasks.title: Complete XML serialization infrastructure
tasks.description:
XML serialization has missing extension methods and type mismatches:
- CS0117: ModelSerializationExtensions.XmlWriterSettings not defined (26 errors)
- CS1929: XmlWriter.WriteObjectValue<T>() extension method not found (10 errors)
- CS0029: Cannot convert List<T> to T[] in XML deserialization (18 errors)

The XML serialization infrastructure files need additional extension methods (XmlWriterSettings, WriteObjectValue) and the XML deserialization code needs to handle List-to-array conversion correctly.

Affected spec: payload/xml.

To reproduce: Remove this spec from test/e2e/.testignore, run pnpm emit:e2e --filter payload/xml, then dotnet build.
acceptance_criteria: ModelSerializationExtensions includes XmlWriterSettings property and WriteObjectValue<T> method
XML deserialization correctly converts List<T> to T[] where needed
payload/xml compiles without errors

phases.title: Golden Output Parity — Sample-TypeSpec Integration Tests
phases.description: Address all differences between the new emitter output and the legacy golden files for the Sample-TypeSpec integration test (test/integration/sample-typespec.test.ts). These tests compare 128 generated C# files against the legacy emitter's golden output. Currently 0/117 common files match, 11 golden files are missing, and 3 extra files are generated. When in doubt, the legacy output wins and should be preserved in the new emitter — the goal is byte-for-byte parity with the golden files.

tasks.title: Remove namespace brace style and header reorder post-processing workarounds
tasks.description:
The emitter currently applies two cosmetic post-processing steps to rendered output:

1. **Namespace brace style** (`src/utils/namespace-brace-style.ts`): Converts K&R-style namespace braces to Allman style via regex after rendering. This works around the Alloy C# `Namespace` component always rendering K&R style.

2. **Header reordering** (`src/utils/reorder-header.ts`): Moves the license/auto-generated header before `using` directives after rendering. This works around the Alloy C# `SourceFile` component rendering usings before children.

Both are cosmetic workarounds tracked in `docs/alloy-issues.md`. Once Alloy supports Allman brace style for namespaces and header ordering in `SourceFile`, these post-processing utilities and their tests should be removed, and the emitter should use the native Alloy features instead.

This task is blocked on upstream Alloy fixes.
acceptance_criteria: src/utils/namespace-brace-style.ts and its test are removed
src/utils/reorder-header.ts and its test are removed
fixAllNamespaceBraceStyles and reorderAllFileHeaders calls removed from src/emitter.tsx
Alloy native features used instead for brace style and header ordering
All scenario tests still pass with correct golden file output

phases.name: E2E Compilation Fixes
phases.description: Fix pre-existing E2E test compilation errors discovered in the Spector test suite

tasks.title: Fix undefined _blob reference in ParamAlias rest client generation
tasks.description: E2E failure: ParamAliasClient.RestClient.cs and IndividuallyNestedWithParamAliasClient.RestClient.cs reference _blob which does not exist in scope. The parameter name resolution for aliased parameters in RestClientFile.tsx is generating an incorrect variable name. Investigate getParamName() / name policy handling in src/components/clients/RestClientFile.tsx.
acceptance_criteria: ParamAliasClient.RestClient.cs compiles without _blob errors
IndividuallyNestedWithParamAliasClient.RestClient.cs compiles without _blob errors
pnpm test:e2e passes for client-initialization specs

tasks.title: Fix undefined Argument helper class in generated model constructors
tasks.description: E2E failure: Error.cs, OperationState.cs, ErrorResponse.cs in azure/core/lro/rpc reference Argument.AssertNotNull() but the Argument helper class is not generated or not in scope. Either src/components/infrastructure/ArgumentFile.tsx is not being emitted, or models referencing it are missing the proper using for the Internal namespace.
acceptance_criteria: Argument helper class is generated in Internal namespace
Models using Argument.AssertNotNull() compile correctly
pnpm test:e2e passes for azure/core/lro/rpc specs

tasks.title: Fix readonly field assignment error in model serialization constructors
tasks.description: E2E failure: OuterModel.cs in azure/client-generator-core/access has CS0191 error — a readonly field is assigned outside of a constructor or init-only setter. The serialization constructor in ModelConstructors.tsx is incorrectly assigning to readonly fields. Investigate whether fields should not be readonly or whether the assignment should be in the constructor.
acceptance_criteria: OuterModel.cs compiles without CS0191 readonly assignment error
Model serialization constructors correctly handle readonly vs mutable fields
pnpm test:e2e passes for access specs

tasks.title: Fix undefined BinaryContentHelper in bytes encoding convenience methods
tasks.description: E2E failure: RequestBody.cs in encode/bytes references BinaryContentHelper which does not exist. The BinaryContentHelper infrastructure class is not being generated. Ensure BinaryContentHelperFile.tsx is included in the emission output. Also check if the file needs to be created or if the component exists but is not wired up.
acceptance_criteria: BinaryContentHelper class is generated
RequestBody.cs compiles without BinaryContentHelper errors
pnpm test:e2e passes for encode/bytes specs

tasks.title: Fix BinaryData-to-string conversion for bytes parameters in headers and queries
tasks.description: E2E failure: Header.RestClient.cs and Query.RestClient.cs in encode/bytes have CS1503 errors — BinaryData cannot convert to string. The getParamValueExpression() in RestClientFile.tsx lacks handling for bytes type parameters in header and query contexts. Need to add Base64 encoding conversion (e.g., Convert.ToBase64String()).
acceptance_criteria: BinaryData parameters in headers are converted to string correctly
BinaryData parameters in queries are converted to string correctly
pnpm test:e2e passes for encode/bytes specs
